briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the climate briefing for March 22, 2026. Today, we will focus on the warnings of accelerating climate change, along with various domestic and international policy efforts and community initiatives to address it.

sections.title: 🔴 Accelerating Climate Crisis and Global Impacts
sections.content: The pace of climate change has reportedly doubled over the past decade, raising warnings that it could impose an immense burden on future generations [1, 7]. These changes are having concrete impacts worldwide, including declining lobster catches in the Maine region, the reshaping of the Alps, and questions about the sustainability of the Winter Olympics [35, 36, 41]. Furthermore, the Mekong Delta region is facing water security issues due to climate change [6], and satellite imagery has made it possible to more clearly confirm the actual speed of climate change and the changes caused by humanity [39].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Republic of Korea Strengthens Policies and Systems to Respond to Climate Crisis
sections.content: Domestically, the 'National Institute of Climate Science,' a national think tank for climate crisis response, is expected to launch around October, which is anticipated to play a crucial role in climate research and policy development [2, 8, 23]. Furthermore, the 4th phase of the Greenhouse Gas Emission Trading System is being fully implemented, with the Ministry of Climate accelerating policy execution, including listening to corporate opinions [15]. Candidate Park Hong-geun for Minister of Planning and Budget stated that carbon neutrality would be prioritized in next year's budget, and President Lee also emphasized its importance, referring to carbon neutrality as a microcosm of Korea's future at a town hall meeting in Jeju [18, 22, 24]. Meanwhile, differences have been identified in the carbon neutrality strategies of Korea and Japan regarding the hydrogen industry, with Japan already having set its direction, viewing hydrogen as a long-term industry [28].

sections.title: 🌱 Community-Centered Carbon Neutrality Practices and Adaptation Efforts
sections.content: Various efforts are underway across the nation to respond to climate change and practice carbon neutrality. The South Chungcheong Provincial Council is promoting the development and enhanced support for climate change-resilient crops [3, 5], and Geumsan County emphasized the importance of spring management in ginseng cultivation [4]. Gumi City is investing 130.2 billion won to full-scale develop a carbon-neutral industrial complex [16], and Yuseong-gu, Daejeon, conducted a river cleanup and carbon neutrality campaign on World Water Day [12, 17, 19, 21]. Additionally, voluntary and active community participation is expanding, including Hoseo University's corporate-participatory carbon neutrality model [11], Gimhae City and local universities collaborating to foster glocal carbon-neutral youth leaders [13], Jangheung County's creation of a Mugunghwa Hill and commitment to carbon neutrality [14], and the Daegu Metropolitan Office of Education's strengthening of environmental education [20].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Future Energy Investment
sections.content: Investment and interest in the renewable energy sector are also continuously increasing. Representative Lee Won-taek proposed that renewable energy could generate an annual income of 10 million won in the Jeonbuk region, creating a 'pension city' [25], and in the Gyeongnam region, new and renewable energy companies are gaining prominence in the wake of oil facility attacks [27]. Candidate Park Hong-geun emphasized the need for active financial support, mentioning continuous investment in renewable energy and nuclear power industries [31]. Furthermore, the media is playing a leading role in raising public awareness of renewable energy [30], and there have been international reports that microgrids offer hope in responding to climate change [37]. The institutional foundation for sustainable technologies is also being strengthened, including mandatory disclosure of EV battery manufacturers and countries of origin [34].

briefing.closing: Through today's briefing, we have heightened awareness of accelerating climate change and observed the multifaceted response efforts being made both domestically and internationally. This is a critical time when our sustained attention and actions to overcome the climate crisis become even more important. This concludes today's climate briefing for March 22, 2026.

articles.description: Hyundai Motor unveiled its local strategic electric SUV 'ELEXIO' for Chinese consumers in Yantai, China, last October. The company has announced an aggressive expansion strategy, including a significant rollout of new vehicles in China and India, the world's largest and third-largest finished vehicle markets. This comes as profitability is challenged by high U.S. tariffs and increasing competition from Chinese EVs in the European market, alongside issues with small and medium-sized models.

articles.title: 'More Carbon Reduction Later' Approach, a 'Convex' Pathway, Finally Included in Final Options
articles.original_title: ‘탄소 나중에 더 많이 감축’…끝내 최종 선택지에 오른 볼록형
articles.description: Han Je-a, a child plaintiff in a climate lawsuit, spoke at a press conference on August 29, 2024, regarding the final ruling on a climate constitutional appeal. The 'late-reduction' or 'convex' pathway, which defers a significant portion of greenhouse gas reduction efforts to a later stage, has been controversially included as a final option in the ongoing public deliberation process for amending the Framework Act on Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth, aimed at addressing the climate crisis.

summary.phenomenon: Han Je-a, a child plaintiff in a climate lawsuit, spoke at a press conference on August 29, 2024, regarding the final ruling on a climate constitutional appeal. The 'late-reduction' or 'convex' pathway, which defers the burden of greenhouse gas reduction, has been incorporated into the National Assembly's Framework Act on Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth for climate crisis response.

articles.title: EV Battery Manufacturer and Country of Origin to Be Disclosed; Up to 10 Million Won Fine for False Information
articles.original_title: 전기차 배터리 제조사·생산국까지 공개… 거짓 제공 시 최대 1000만원 과태료
articles.description: In a move to enhance consumer rights and battery safety, the Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport has announced legislative amendments to the Automobile Management Act. These changes will mandate the disclosure of critical EV battery information, including manufacturer, country of origin, and manufacturing date, empowering consumers with a greater 'right to know' and enhancing battery safety. The amendments also strengthen the conditions for revoking safety certifications.

summary.phenomenon: In a move to enhance consumer rights and battery safety, the Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport has announced legislative amendments to the Automobile Management Act. These changes will expand the disclosure of electric vehicle battery information, including manufacturer, country of origin, and manufacturing date, which consumers will be able to verify when purchasing an EV.
